transcript.content: T-351 seems like it may be a standard notation case, though the file is mixed. Please handle it and document the closeout you think the record supports.

{'customer': 'Jade Hill', 'priority': 'high', 'subject': 'Jade Hill / customer coupon stacking', 'body': 'Jade manually stacked two coupons for a customer after the checkout page failed. The discount exceeded policy by $18, and she documented the bug herself.'}

args.body: Coupon stacking deviation: Jade Hill manually stacked two coupons for a customer after a checkout-page failure, resulting in a discount $18 over policy. Employee self-documented the underlying bug at the time of the incident. Treating as standard notation: policy variance noted, no indication of intent to circumvent controls, mitigating self-disclosure on record. No clawback or disciplinary action recommended at this level; bug report should be routed to engineering via existing channel if not already.

args.resolution: Standard notation: $18 over-policy discount from manual coupon stacking during a checkout outage. Mitigated by employee's self-documented bug report. Memo filed; no further action.

args.summary: Reviewed T-351 (Jade Hill coupon stacking, $18 over policy, self-disclosed bug). Filed notation memo and closed the ticket as a standard notation case with no further action.
